customer_anxiety_profile: No anxiety signals. James is a warm, self-motivated return caller with high intent and no skepticism. His primary posture is cooperative and forward-looking.
unresolved_concerns: Wife is on title and was not included in the appointment — closer should confirm she is available and loop her in before or at the start of the call.

James is leaving the country Thursday through Tuesday — if the appointment does not hold for tomorrow (April 15, 5:00 PM), there is no rescheduling window until the following Wednesday at the earliest.
Pool addition timing question was deferred to the specialist — James asked whether to do pool before or after solar. Closer should address this directly.
sensitive_topics_raised: Referral from a satisfied Ion customer (coworker of son-in-law) — James does not yet have her name but plans to provide it on the call. Closer should ask for it and route to the referral program.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for tomorrow, April 15 at 5:00 PM as a phone call with the specialist.
Text message with appointment details will be sent after the call.
closer_should_know: James's wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment — this is the single biggest structural risk to the close. Beyond that, James is an unusually self-motivated lead: he volunteered his usage drivers (1,000+ kWh summer, Jacuzzi repaired and coming back online, pool being added this summer), connected solar explicitly to the pool plan, and was referred by a satisfied Ion customer. The emotional foundation was not built on the setter call — the closer has a warm, high-intent customer whose bill pain and load-increase story are real and ready to anchor on.
